source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Image Report/011_Clinical Image Report  - F1417MCW.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for F1417MCW against HAD1417MCW using plain radiographic images acquired in DICOM 3.0.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by comparing image quality, anatomy visibility, and clinical conclusions for F1417MCW and HAD1417MCW.
keyPoints: Objective device is F1417MCW by H&abyz and comparative equipment is HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz.
The report uses DICOM 3.0 output data for downloading and analysis.
Thirty radiographic views were evaluated with S, A, B, C grading.
Total average scores were 3.63 for HAD1417MCW and 3.65 for F1417MCW.
The conclusion states F1417MCW images are comparable or superior to HAD1417MCW images.

concepts.evidence: After comparing a broad review of plain radiographic images taken with the F1417MCW and the HAD1417MCW

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation expert qualification
concepts.evidence: The selected clinical evaluation expert, Professor Sung Hyun Kim, M.D., Ph.D., is a board-certified radiologist in Korea with over 30 years of clinical, academic, and research experience

evidence.claim: The document identifies F1417MCW as the objective device.
The document identifies HAD1417MCW as the comparative equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Comparative Equipment : HAD1417MCW made by H&Abyz

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev. No 0.
The document revision date is Dec/22/2022.
The document uses DICOM 3.0 for output data formatting, downloading, and analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: output data for communications standard DICOM 3.0 is used to format at the data for downloading and analysis

evidence.claim: Thirty radiographic views were summarized with average values for HAD1417MCW and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Average Value of each annotation No Contents HAD1417MCW F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The total average score was 3.63 for HAD1417MCW and 3.65 for F1417MCW.
The report concludes F1417MCW images are comparable or superior to HAD1417MCW images.
evidence.evidenceText: the images obtained with the F1417MCW are comparable or superior to the same view obtained from a similar patient with the HAD1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The report states spatial and soft tissue contrast resolution are superior using F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: In general, both the spatial and soft tissue contrast resolution are superior using the F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation expert is described as satisfying an FDA requirement for evaluation by a U.S. radiologist or equivalently qualified expert.
evidence.evidenceText: His qualifications and professional history are equivalent to those of a U.S. board-certified radiologist, fulfilling the requirement set forth by the FDA

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is not presented as an issued approval or FDA document; FDA appears only in the expert qualification justification.
Document type is inferred from the source path filename because the text header uses Clinical Consideration 3 rather than Clinical Image Report.
Text includes OCR or transcription quality issues such as DCIOM, Sternal Angel, and Insperction.
